content:
# Acts Chapter 13 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 240-242 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_013_page_001.jpg, ACT_013_page_002.jpg, ACT_013_page_003.jpg

---

**1** Now there were in Antioch, in the existing church, prophets and teachers: both Barnabas and Symeon the [one] being-called Niger, and Lucius the Cyrenian, and Manaen [who was] a foster-brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

**2** And [while] they were ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, "Set-apart indeed for me both Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them."

**3** Then having fasted and having prayed and having laid the hands upon them, they released [them].

**4** They therefore, having been sent out by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia, and from-there they sailed-away to Cyprus.

**5** And having come-to-be in Salamis, they were proclaiming the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews; and they were having also John [as] an attendant.

**6** And having passed-through the whole island as-far-as Paphos, they found a certain man, a sorcerer, a false-prophet, a Jew, whose name [was] Bar-Jesus,

**7** who was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man. This [one], having summoned Barnabas and Saul, sought to hear the word of God.

**8** But Elymas the sorcerer was opposing them -- for so is his name being translated -- seeking to turn-away the proconsul from the faith.

**9** But Saul, the [one] also [called] Paul, having been filled [with the] Holy Spirit and having gazed-intently at him,

**10** said, "O [you] full of all deceit and all villainy, son of [the] devil, enemy of all righteousness, will you not cease perverting the straight ways of the Lord?

**11** And now behold, [the] hand of the Lord [is] upon you, and you will be blind, not seeing the sun until a season." And immediately there fell upon him a mist and a darkness, and going-about he was seeking [ones to] lead [him] by-the-hand.

**12** Then the proconsul, having seen the [thing] having happened, believed, being-astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

**13** And having been brought-out-to-sea from Paphos, those around Paul came to Perga of Pamphylia; but John, having departed from them, returned to Jerusalem.

**14** And they, having passed-through from Perga, arrived at Antioch the Pisidian, and having entered into the synagogue on the day of the sabbaths, they sat-down.

**15** And after the reading of the law and the prophets, the synagogue-rulers sent to them, saying, "Men, brothers, if there is any word in you of exhortation to the people, speak."

**16** And Paul, having stood-up and having motioned with the hand, said, "Men, Israelites, and the [ones] fearing God, hear.

**17** The God of this people Israel chose our fathers, and the people he exalted in the sojourn in [the] land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm he led them out of it.

**18** And for a time of about forty years he bore-with-them in the wilderness.

**19** And having destroyed seven nations in [the] land of Canaan, he gave-as-an-inheritance their land --

**20** for about four-hundred and fifty years. And after these [things] he gave judges until Samuel the prophet.

**21** And from-then they asked-for a king, and God gave to them Saul, [the] son of Kish, a man of [the] tribe of Benjamin, [for] forty years.

**22** And having removed him, he raised-up David for them as king, to whom also he said, having testified, 'I found David the [son] of Jesse, a man according-to my heart, who will do all my desires.'

**23** Of this [man's] seed, God according-to promise brought to Israel a savior, Jesus,

**24** John having previously-proclaimed before [the] face of his entrance a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.

**25** And as John was fulfilling the course, he was saying, 'What do you suppose me to be? I am not [he]. But behold, [one] comes after me, of whom I am not worthy [to] loose the sandal of [his] feet.'

**26** Men, brothers, sons of [the] family of Abraham, and the [ones] among you fearing God, to us the word of this salvation was sent-out.

**27** For the [ones] dwelling in Jerusalem and their rulers, not-having-known this [one], and the voices of the prophets the [ones] being read according-to every sabbath, having judged [him], they fulfilled [them].

**28** And having found no cause of death, they asked Pilate [for] him to be killed.

**29** And when they finished all the [things] having been written concerning him, having taken-down [him] from the tree, they placed [him] in a tomb.

**30** But God raised him from [the] dead;

**31** who appeared for many days to the [ones] having come-up-with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who now are his witnesses to the people.

**32** And we are bringing-good-news to you -- the promise having been made to the fathers --

**33** that God has fulfilled this to us, the children of them, having raised-up Jesus, as also in the second psalm it has been written, 'You are my Son, today I have begotten you.'

**34** And that he raised him from [the] dead, no-longer being-about to return to corruption, thus he has spoken: 'I will give to you the holy [things] of David, the faithful [things].'

**35** Therefore also in another [psalm] he says, 'You will not give your Holy-One to see corruption.'

**36** For David indeed, having served [the] counsel of God in his own generation, fell-asleep, and was added to his fathers, and saw corruption;

**37** but [he] whom God raised-up did not see corruption.

**38** Therefore let it be known to you, men, brothers, that through this [one] forgiveness of sins is being proclaimed to you,

**39** and from all [things] from which you were not able to be justified in [the] law of Moses, in this [one] everyone believing is justified.

**40** Watch therefore, lest the [thing] having been spoken in the prophets should come-upon [you]:

**41** 'Behold, the despisers, and marvel, and perish, because a work I am working in your days, a work which you will by-no-means believe, even-if someone should declare [it] to you.'"

**42** And [as] they were going-out, they were urging [that] these words be spoken to them on the next sabbath.

**43** And [the] synagogue having been dismissed, many of the Jews and the worshipping proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas, who, speaking to them, were persuading them to continue in the grace of God.

**44** And on the coming sabbath, nearly all the city was gathered to hear the word of God.

**45** But the Jews, having seen the crowds, were filled with jealousy and were contradicting the [things] being spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.

**46** And having spoken-boldly, both Paul and Barnabas said, "To you it was necessary first [for] the word of God to be spoken. Since you are thrusting it away and are judging yourselves not worthy of eternal life, behold, we are turning to the nations.

**47** For thus the Lord has commanded us: 'I have set you for a light of nations, [for] you to be for salvation unto [the] end of the earth.'"

**48** And hearing [this], the nations were rejoicing and were glorifying the word of the Lord, and believed -- as-many-as were having been appointed to eternal life.

**49** And the word of the Lord was being carried through the whole region.

**50** But the Jews stirred-up the worshipping women, the prominent [ones], and the chief-men of the city, and raised-up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and cast them out from their boundaries.

**51** And they, having shaken-off the dust of the feet against them, came to Iconium.

**52** And the disciples were being filled with joy and [the] Holy Spirit.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**Acts 13 -- Codex Sinaiticus Observations**

1. **Verse 1 -- "Symeon":** Sinaiticus reads CYMEΩN (Symeon), preserving the Semitic form. Later manuscripts often render this as "Simon."

2. **Verse 6 -- "Bar-Jesus" (ΒΑΡΙΗCΟΥΝ):** The Sinaiticus spelling of the name reflects an alternative transliteration of the Aramaic patronymic.

3. **Verse 8 -- "Elymas":** Sinaiticus preserves ΕΛΥΜΑC. The parenthetical explanation ("for so is his name being translated") remains a scholarly puzzle, as "Elymas" does not straightforwardly translate "Bar-Jesus." It may derive from an Arabic root for "wise man" / "sorcerer."

4. **Verse 13 -- "those around Paul":** Sinaiticus reads ΟΙ ΠΕΡΙ ΠΑΥΛΟΝ ("those around Paul"), a construction that can mean "Paul and his companions." This marks the shift in narrative where Paul assumes leadership over the missionary party. Some manuscripts read "Paul and those with him."

5. **Verse 18 -- "bore-with-them" (ΕΤΡΟΠΟΦΟΡΗCΕΝ):** Sinaiticus reads ΕΤΡΟΠΟΦΟΡΗCΕΝ (etropophoresn, "he bore their manner/conduct"). A well-known variant is ΕΤΡΟΦΟΦΟΡΗCΕΝ (etrophophoresn, "he nourished/sustained them"). The difference is a single letter (π vs. φ), and both readings have strong manuscript support. The Sinaiticus reading emphasizes God's patient endurance of Israel's waywardness.

6. **Verse 19 -- Timing clause:** The conjunction of verses 19-20 regarding the 450 years is notoriously difficult. Sinaiticus places the "about four-hundred and fifty years" after the distribution of the land, connecting it to the period of the judges, following the Western text arrangement.

7. **Verse 20 -- "the prophet":** Sinaiticus includes ΠΡΟΦΗΤΟΥ after Samuel, reading "Samuel the prophet."

8. **Verse 23 -- "brought" vs. "raised up":** Sinaiticus reads ΗΓΑΓΕΝ ("brought/led"), confirming the reading that God "brought" a savior from David's seed, rather than the variant "raised up" (ΗΓΕΙΡΕΝ) found in some later witnesses.

9. **Verse 33 -- "the children of them":** Sinaiticus reads ΤΟΙC ΤΕΚΝΟΙC ΑΥΤΩΝ ("to the children of them"), where some manuscripts add ΗΜΙΝ ("to us, their children"). The Sinaiticus text distinguishes "us" as the recipients from the fathers' children as the beneficiaries.

10. **Verse 33 -- "second psalm":** Sinaiticus reads ΔΕΥΤΕΡΩ ("second"), confirming the psalm reference. Some manuscripts read FIRST (ΠΡΩΤΩ), reflecting an ancient variant where Psalms 1 and 2 were counted as a single psalm.

11. **Verse 41 -- "Behold, the despisers":** Sinaiticus reads ΚΑΤΑΦΡΟΝΗΤΑΙ ("despisers"), from Habakkuk 1:5. The LXX text from which this is quoted has variant forms; some manuscripts read "among the nations" which Sinaiticus appears to omit.

12. **Verse 42 -- Assembly departure:** Sinaiticus text at this transition verse is slightly damaged but the reading supports "as they were going out" (ΕΞΙΟΝΤΩΝ ΑΥΤΩΝ), without the longer Western addition specifying that it was the Jews who departed and the Gentiles who asked.

13. **Verse 44 -- "word of God":** Sinaiticus reads ΛΟΓΟΝ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ ("word of God"). The Byzantine text tradition reads ΛΟΓΟΝ ΤΟΥ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("word of the Lord"). This variation between "God" and "Lord" recurs throughout Acts in Sinaiticus.

14. **Verse 48 -- "were having been appointed":** The periphrastic perfect ΗCΑΝ ΤΕΤΑΓΜΕΝΟΙ preserves the force of divine prior-appointment; this is not "those who appointed themselves" but "those who had been appointed" -- the passive voice indicating divine action.

-- EMET
